title: The Android component lifecycle
description: Activities and Fragments are created, destroyed, and recreated on configuration changes and memory pressure, so their instances are short-lived.
body: Use when reasoning about why state vanishes or leaks. The system drives Activities/Fragments through create/start/resume/pause/stop/destroy, and recreates them on configuration changes (rotation, locale, theme) and can destroy them under memory pressure; a new instance is built each time. Do not treat an Activity instance as a stable home for state or hold long references to it. Actions: scope durable state to a ViewModel and observe it. Validation signals include state surviving recreation. Risk boundary: storing state in the Activity loses it on every config change, and capturing an Activity/Context in a long-lived object leaks it across recreation — instance lifetime is short and system-controlled, design around it.
